assertions: Output begins with the literal line `Findings:`
All eight preamble fields are present in order
Findings are tagged with severity in backticks (`High`, `Medium`, `Low`, or `Critical` if a higher tier is used)
The use of POST sub-resource verb (`/api/tasks/:id/complete`) is identified as inconsistent with REST conventions — should be PATCH on the resource
The missing Idempotency-Key on the side-effectful POST is identified as a finding
The snake_case wire field `task_id` is identified as a finding (should be camelCase)
The `200` status (rather than reflecting the state-transition correctly) and the bypass of the canonical error envelope are noted where relevant
Findings are ordered by severity, highest first
Output does not cite either skill by name (no mention of 'structured-code-review' or 'rest-api-design' as the source of any rule)
